calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Continued To Dec-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Kelly appeared via zoom for and with Martha Turnage (petitioner).
Melanie Emmons appeared via zoom (Court appointed counsel).

Mr. Kelly stated that he paid the sanctions today, that they only let him pay $435.00. The Court waives the remaining $65.00.

Ms. Emmons stated that they are opening a conservatorship matter in Contra Costa County and would request a continuance.

The matter is continued to December 8, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To Jun-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for administrator Martha Turnage, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for status on close of estate.

Mr. Kelly addressed the status of the administration. The Court directed Mr. Kelly to file a petition pursuant to Probate Code section 12200 and inquired why such a petition had not been filed.

The Court addressed bond. Mr. Kelly stated that his client is of limited means, has no current cash assets, and intends to seek relief from bond and future filing fees.

The Court further addressed previously ordered sanctions that remain unpaid. Mr. Kelly was unable to provide an explanation.

The Court continued the matter to June 2, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and directed counsel to file a petition to approve report of status of administration on file prior to that date. The Court stated that an order to show cause shall issue requiring Mr. Kelly to appear and show cause why additional sanctions in the amount of $250 should not be imposed for failure to pay sanctions as previously ordered. The Court ordered that the outstanding sanctions shall be paid prior to the continued hearing and indicated that if paid, the order to show cause will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-16-2025 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Mark Kelly appeared via zoom for and with Martha Turnage (petitioner).

Mr. Kelly stated that the unpaid sanctions were an oversight, that he would take care of payment soon, that there is currently a conservatorship matter with a partition action being sought, that there is interest in a property located in San Francisco, and that Ms. Turnage is unable to pay the bond and is seeking to place a lien on the property to secure a loan.

The matter is continued to April 7, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-16-2025 Continued To Dec-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for administrator Martha Turnage, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on status of close of estate.

The Court noted that previously imposed sanctions from the June 17, 2025 hearing had not yet been paid. Mr. Kelly stated that he mistakenly believed he had paid them but would take the necessary steps to ensure payment.

Mr. Kelly reported that the sole asset of the estate if s one-fifth interest in a residence in San Francisco, with the remainder owned by heirs Carrie Carter and Bertha Turnage. Ms. Carter has taken exclusive possession of the property, and a partition action will likely need to be filed in order to address issues related to code violations and liability. Bertha Turnage had agreed to join as a co-litigant in the partition action but was subsequently diagnosed with progressive dementia.

Mr. Kelly stated that Bertha Turnage recently executed a limited power of attorney in favor of her son, but it does not authorize him to pursue the partition action, and that he believes a conservatorship proceeding is necessary for proper representation in the civil matter.

After discussing possible next steps, the Court continued the matter to December 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, for further status.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for and with Martha Turnage, Administrator (via Zoom).

The Court called the matter on two pending items: the petition for approval of status report and the order to show cause regarding failure to appear at the prior hearing. Mr. Kelly acknowledged that the prior missed hearing resulted from a calendaring oversight and accepted the Court's imposition of $250 in sanctions.

The Court noted two unresolved issues from the examiner's notes: no notice of hearing was filed, and no proposed order was submitted. The Court took the current petition off calendar as moot and set a status hearing on close of estate for September 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Mr. Kelly provided a case update. The estate holds a one-fifth interest in real property. The remaining four-fifths interest is divided between two additional heirs, one of whom-Bertha Turnage, petitioner's sister-had intended to join the petition to partition and sell the property. However, Ms. Turnage suffered a series of mini-strokes and is now in a memory care facility. She no longer has capacity. Her son, who resides out of state, is seeking appointment as her conservator in Contra Costa County, but no petition has yet been filed. The hope is that, with conservatorship in place, the partition action may proceed with a majority of the co-owners aligned.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear [Re: Administrator Martha Turnage]
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Off Calendar

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Continued To Jun-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Mark Kelly (attorney) to appear on June 17, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear. An order to Appear will be issued to Martha Turnage (petitioner) to appear on June 17, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Petition to Approve Status Report and to Continue Administrator is also continued to that date.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Thompson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Kelly appeared by zoom for Martha Turnage (administrator).

The court that Mr. Kelly was supposed to have filed his petition by the 15th and questions what happened. Mr. Kelly stated that he filed a partition action, that he is attempting service and having some issues with the party who is living in the property, that there are multiple interest holders and that they prefer cash rather than a fractional interest. Status on filing of a report on status of administration is off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Colhour. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-01-2024 Continued To Oct-22-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Kelly appeared by zoom for Martha Turnage (administrator).

The court noted that the September 3rd minutes indicate that Mr. Kelly was to file a petition for approval of a report on status of administration by September 24th and questions why nothing was filed. Mr. Kelly stated that he was filing a partition action in the civil court, that he needs to have other parties served, that the civil case has not been returned to him by the clerk and that he does not have a case number. The court informed Mr. Kelly that he needs to file a petition/status report in this case that provides all of the required information and that he needs to provide the civil case number. Status on filing of a petition for approval of a report on status of administration is continued to October 22, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Kelly is to have his petition filed by then.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Gutierrez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Continued To Oct-01-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Kelly appeared zoom for Martha Turnage (administrator).

Mr. Kelly stated that he has been in communication with the various intestate heirs and the third-party owners of the property, and that all of these individuals had initially agreed that the property would be sold and that the intestate heirs would be receiving cash distributions for their interests, but communications have broken down. Mr. Kelly also stated that his client is looking to do a partition action, that he has seen the letter that his client sent to the court and that the bond premiums have been paid. The court informed Mr. Kelly that it did not read the letter as it is considered ex parte communication. Mr. Kelly is to file a petition for approval of a report on status of administration by September 24, 2024. Status on close of estate is continued to October 1, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Lopez. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Mark Kelly, via Zoom, for Martha Turnage, via Zoom, Petitioner; Bertha Turnage, in court, in propria persona, Objector; Johnny Carter and Carrie Carter, in court, in propria persona, Interested Parties.

Discussion was held regarding receiving checks for Ms. Carter, that the city property building inspector is sending notices for maintenance of the property, and that the property taxes are not being paid. Mr. Kelly stated he would look into the four items mentioned in court, that there was an error in Lucille Carter's interest, and that it should be 2/5 intrest instead of 1/3 interest. Administrators petition for instructions regarding interest of estate in real property is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Martha Turnage and the Attorney Mark L. Kelly to appear on June 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear, and why either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. An order to show cause re sanctions is also being issued for the same date for Attorney Mark L. Kelly to appear and show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear pursuant to CCP 128. PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Kelly, via Zoom, for Martha Turnage, via Zoom, Petitioner; Bertha Turnage, in court, in propria persona, Objector; Johnny Carter and Carrie Carter, in court, in propria persona, Interested Parties.

The court acknowledges Bertha Turnage's objections that were formally filed and advises the other persons in court that the court will only consider objections if they are formally filed. Mr. Carter reports of not having formally received notice of this action. The court reminds all parties of the rules of the court regarding proper service. The court asks Johnny and Carrie if they are requesting a continuance to allow them to formally file objections. They both advise that they had intended to formally file objections and state their reasons why they have not done so. The parties offer oral testimony regarding the real property located at 182 Quesada Avenue, San Francisco, and of the percentages of ownership thereof, and that Carrie Carter currently resides there.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Letters of Administration is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Glisson. Not reported.
